content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks constitute a three-stage selection funnel that removes academically able students from the standard 8-year basic school system at ages 10, 12, and 14, with an additional selection point at school entry (age 7).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Describes structural mechanism only, does not establish causal effects on outcomes
Does not quantify proportion of students affected by each selection point
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
